meta.purpose: Catalog of all proven task patterns, tool combinations, and multi-step workflows for the Nemotron agent. Every schema is a crystallized lesson. Missing tools in any schema = a gap to fix.
meta.rule: If a new task type is proven, add it here. If a schema lacks a tool, add that tool to the pipeline.

read_file.desc: Read file content with optional line range

tree.desc: Full directory tree to configurable depth

find_files.desc: Find files by pattern/extension across subtree

new_file.desc: Create new file — ALWAYS verify after with list_dir

edit_file.desc: Exact string replacement in existing file

run_cmd.desc: Run shell command — NOT python -c on Windows

lookup_book.desc: Search Book of Luis concept graph (690 nodes)

schemas.name: Document Generation
schemas.description: Research a topic via book + codebase, then write a structured markdown document.
lessons: Use lookup_book FIRST to find prior art in the concept graph
Read source files for accuracy before writing
new_file MUST be followed by list_dir to verify
Structure document: Overview → Detail sections → Summary
schemas.example_prompt: Write a document called {NAME}.md explaining {TOPIC}. Use lookup_book to research first. Create with new_file, verify with list_dir.

schemas.name: Codebase Exploration + Architecture Map
schemas.description: Explore an unknown codebase, understand structure, produce an architecture summary.
lessons: Start with tree at depth=3 to get full structural overview
Use find_files with extensions to locate specific file types
Read entry points first (index.py, main.py, __init__.py)
search_code for key patterns (class, def, import) to map dependencies
schemas.example_prompt: Explore the {REPO} codebase. Use tree to understand structure, then map all Python modules and their relationships. Write ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md.

schemas.name: Bug Diagnosis + Fix
schemas.description: Given a failing test or error output, diagnose root cause and implement minimal fix.
lessons: Read the failing code BEFORE attempting a fix
Run the test first to confirm the failure
Fix root cause, not symptom
After edit_file, run test again to verify fix
If same error appears twice — stop, re-read, find deeper cause
Do NOT use python -c on Windows — write a .py file instead
schemas.example_prompt: Fix the failing test in {FILE}. Error: {ERROR_MSG}. Diagnose root cause, implement minimal fix, re-run test to confirm.
schemas.proven_on: XML closing tag fix, memory.py Pico layer analysis
schemas.gap: needs multi-file consistency check after fix

schemas.name: Schema Generation from Source
schemas.description: Read source code/config files and generate a formal JSON Schema, OpenAPI spec, or architecture schema.
lessons: Use tree + find_files to locate all relevant source files first
Read __init__.py and base classes to understand the interface
Cross-reference with lookup_book for design patterns
Output JSON Schema with $schema, $id, title, description, properties, required
Validate: schema must reference actual field names from source
schemas.example_prompt: Generate a JSON Schema for {COMPONENT}. Read the source with tree + find_files, then write {NAME}_schema.json.

schemas.name: Image to SVG Conversion (Two-Stage NIM Pipeline)
schemas.description: Convert diagram images to SVG using vision model (analysis) + reasoning model (SVG generation).
lessons: Use gatekeeper diagram_to_svg --image PATH --output PATH
Process smallest files first as test batch
Verify output SVG exists with list_dir after each conversion
Description JSON is saved alongside SVG as intermediate artifact
Skip files >500KB for first pass (vision model token cost)
schemas.example_prompt: Convert blueprints/images/{IMAGE}.jpg to blueprints/svg/{IMAGE}.svg using the diagram_to_svg tool.

schemas.name: New GatekeeperTool Creation
schemas.description: Create a new tool as a GatekeeperTool class with .py + .json manifest pair.
lessons: lookup_book first to check if a similar tool exists in the book
Read gatekeeper/base.py for GatekeeperTool interface
Read an existing tool (route.py) as pattern reference
new_file closing tag MUST be </tool> not </tool_file>
Immediately verify new file exists with list_dir
Manifest .json needs: name, purpose, motive, data_consumed, data_produced
schemas.example_prompt: Create a new GatekeeperTool called {NAME} that {DESCRIPTION}. Read base.py and route.py as patterns first.
schemas.gap: XML closing tag bug causes silent failure — verify after create

schemas.name: Benchmark + Performance Analysis
schemas.description: Run a benchmark, analyze results, identify gaps, write improvement plan.
lessons: Read the benchmark script before running
Write results to a .py test file — do NOT use python -c
Compare baseline vs improved metric explicitly
Document failures with: query / expected / got / root_cause
Always propose next improvement at end
schemas.example_prompt: Run scratch/benchmark_memory.py --compare, analyze the failures, propose and implement one improvement to the Pico layer.

schemas.name: Session Memory Write + Load Cycle
schemas.description: At session end: compress to BRAID. At session start: load relevant context via graph-RAG.
lessons: memory load --query TASK_DESCRIPTION at session start
memory write --was X --wasnt Y --canbe Z at session end
BRAID entries are ~40 tokens each, bounded regardless of history
Graph-RAG retrieves concept graph context, not just session history
schemas.example_prompt: Before starting task, run: gatekeeper memory load --query '{TASK}'. After completing, run: gatekeeper memory write --was '...' --wasnt '...' --canbe '...'

schemas.name: Multi-File Refactor with Consistency Check
schemas.description: Rename a function/class across multiple files, maintaining consistency.
lessons: search_code to find ALL occurrences before touching anything
Edit files in dependency order (define before use)
Run tests or syntax check after each file edit
Keep a mental map of what's been changed
schemas.example_prompt: Rename {OLD_NAME} to {NEW_NAME} across all files in {PATH}. Use search_code to find all occurrences first.
schemas.gap: no multi-file consistency tracker — needs plan tool

schemas.name: Continuous Batch Operation (Non-Stop)
schemas.description: Process N items in a loop without stopping. E.g. convert 30 images, process 690 nodes.
lessons: Use --max-turns 40+ for long batch operations
Process in sorted order so progress is resumable
Check output exists before processing (skip already done)
Log progress to a .jsonl file so session can resume if interrupted
Start with smallest/simplest items to validate pipeline
schemas.example_prompt: Process all {N} items in {INPUT_DIR}. Skip already-processed ones. Write output to {OUTPUT_DIR}. Log progress to progress.jsonl.
schemas.proven_on: pending — first test: 30 blueprint images → SVGs

tool_coverage_matrix.note: Which schemas use which tools. Gaps = tools not exercised.

gaps.tree_in_S01: Document generation should start with tree to understand repo context
gaps.memory_in_all: Every schema should end with memory write (session BRAID)

pending_operations.task: Generate JSON Schema for the GatekeeperTool registry
Implement hybrid search (semantic + keyword) in memory.py Pico layer
Create plan.py tool — multi-step plan tracking with progress
pending_operations.pattern: Write plan → mark steps done → verify all complete
